mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the last quarter signals an easing discount-rate regime that lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive in easing episodes, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03 while FEDFUNDS is declining.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is still near the lower bound, real rates stay compressed and the market prices continued accommodation, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises faster than FEDFUNDS for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-policy trigger does not produce positive 60d returns more often than not, or if the signal has Rank IC below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and slower demand, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by at least 0.3 percentage points from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the effect disappears for two consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: Declining PAYEMS indicates weakening payroll growth and lower aggregate income, which should transmit to weaker corporate revenue expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 3 consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Reject if three-month payroll declines do not precede negative 60d returns with at least 55% hit rate, or if Rank IC is persistently below 0.03 over any 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in RSAFS signals stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising retail sales do not correspond to positive 20d SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the effect flips negative for an extended rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in INDPRO and HOUST indicates an improving supply-side expansion that reduces recession risk and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in industrial production and housing starts fail to outperform unconditional returns over 60d, or if the interaction term loses significance in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in GDPC1 after a prior expansion phase marks demand recession, and equity markets should discount weaker earnings with negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines from the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP contractions are not followed by negative 60d SPX returns with a clear majority of occurrences, or if the signal fails to beat a zero benchmark in rolling backtests.

mechanism_narrative: During an aggressive tightening cycle, a high FEDFUNDS level combined with rising CPIAUCSL implies restrictive real financial conditions that compress multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still rising year over year
failure_condition: Reject if the tightening trigger does not produce negative 20d returns in at least 55% of triggered cases, or if the interaction signal's Rank IC remains below 0.03 across any 2-year rolling window.
